methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

private.name: Seasats
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Havoc AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A–stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau that has raised $63M to date. The private comp universe for early-stage frontier ocean tech is limited, comprising two identified peers: Seasats and Havoc AI. No public comparables exist in this nascent sector, constraining the ability to apply traditional trading or transaction multiples.

The valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the company's recent capital raise. Using total funds raised ($63M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to subsequent funding rounds yields an implied valuation of $126M. This reflects the expected value accretion between Series A and later stages, absent material changes in risk profile or market conditions.

The triangulated range is $107M (low) to $145M (high), with a median of $126M. The relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological anchor; the wide dispersion of outcomes for frontier-tech ventures and the minimal public market precedent create material uncertainty around any point estimate. Key risks to valuation include execution risk in a capital-intensive sector, regulatory evolution affecting maritime technology deployment, and limited exit comparables. Additional methods—including revenue multiple analysis or discounted cash flow—would require operational and financial data not provided in this analysis.
